2604.16138.title: Sentiment Analysis of German Sign Language Fairy Tales
2604.16138.paper_summary: We present a dataset and a model for sentiment analysis of German sign language (DGS) fairy tales. First, we perform sentiment analysis for three levels of valence (negative, neutral, positive) on German fairy tales text segments using four large language models (LLMs) and majority voting, reaching an inter-annotator agreement of 0.781 Krippendorff's alpha. Second, we extract face and body motion features from each corresponding DGS video segment using MediaPipe. Finally, we train an explainable model (based on XGBoost) to predict negative, neutral or positive sentiment from video features. Results show an average balanced accuracy of 0.631. A thorough analysis of the most important features reveal that, in addition to eyebrows and mouth motion on the face, also the motion of hips, elbows, and shoulders considerably contribute in the discrimination of the conveyed sentiment, indicating an equal importance of face and body for sentiment communication in sign language.
2604.16138.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個數據集和一個模型，用於德國手語（DGS）童話的情感分析。<lb/>首先，我們對德國童話文本片段進行三個情感價值層次（負面、中性、正面）的情感分析，使用四個大型語言模型（LLMs）和多數投票，達到0.781的Krippendorff α一致性。<lb/>其次，我們使用MediaPipe從每個相應的DGS視頻片段中提取面部和身體運動特徵。<lb/>最後，我們訓練了一個可解釋的模型（基於XGBoost），以從視頻特徵預測負面、中性或正面的情感。<lb/>結果顯示平均平衡準確率為0.631。<lb/>對最重要特徵的徹底分析顯示，除了面部的眉毛和嘴巴運動外，臀部、肘部和肩部的運動也在傳達的情感識別中有顯著貢獻，表明在手語的情感傳遞中，面部和身體同樣重要。
2604.16138.author: Fabrizio Nunnari et.al.
2604.16138.authors: Fabrizio Nunnari, Siddhant Jain, Patrick Gebhard
